%Text automatically generated by txt2latex
\section*{DESCRIPTION}
\emph{codata} is a Fortran library providing the fundamental physical constants
published by CODATA. 
It includes the 2022, 2018, 2014 and 2010 recommended values, 
allowing applications to reproduce calculations based on specific 
CODATA releases.
  
The constants are implemented as a derived type that carries:
\begin{itemize}
\item the name
\item the value 
\item the uncertainty 
\item the unit
\end{itemize}
  
All the constants are provided as double precision reals.
Long constant names can be aliased with shorter names.

\section*{FEATURES}
It provides:
\begin{itemize}
\item CODATA 2022 constants
\item CODATA 2018 constants
\item CODATA 2014 constants
\item CODATA 2010 constants
\item Native Fortran API
\item C interoperability API
\item Python bindings
\end{itemize}

\section*{DOCUMENTATION }
The API is written as POSIX-style man pages which are generated from the
sources using the prep(1) preprocessor and are distributed with the binaries. 
  
The reference manual provides complete documentation:
\begin{description}
\item[PDF] \href{https://milanskocic.github.io/codata/latex/codata-refman.pdf}{milanskocic.github.io/codata/latex/codata-refman.pdf} 
\item[HTML] \href{https://milanskocic.github.io/codata/index.html}{milanskocic.github.io/codata/index.html} 
\end{description}
  
API only is also available as a bookman:
\begin{description}
\item[MAN] \href{https://milanskocic.github.io/codata/man}{milanskocic.github.io/codata/man} 
\item[PDF] \href{https://milanskocic.github.io/codata/man/codata-bookman.pdf}{milanskocic.github.io/codata/man/codata-bookman.pdf} 
\end{description}

\section*{NOTES FOR FORTRAN USERS}
The \emph{codata} constants from the 2022 adjustment were integrated into the Fortran 
standard library (stdlib).
This library is complementary to the constants defined in the stdlib
by providing older values for the constants.
See \href{https://github.com/fortran-lang/stdlib/releases/tag/v0.7.0}{github.com/fortran-lang/stdlib/releases/tag/v0.7.0.} 
If your application only requires the latest CODATA values, the
Fortran standard library may be sufficient. Use \emph{codata} when you need
access to historical CODATA releases or compatibility with older
scientific results.
  
To use \emph{codata} within your fpm(1) (\href{https://github.com/fortran-lang/fpm}{github.com/fortran-lang/fpm)} 
project, add the following lines to your file:

\begin{verbatim}
     [dependencies]
     codata = { git="https://github.com/MilanSkocic/codata.git" }

\end{verbatim}
If you only need sources for the CODATA constants that can be 
integrated directly into your project, you may be interested in the 
following repository:
\href{https://github.com/vmagnin/fundamental\_constants}{github.com/vmagnin/fundamental\_constants.}
